focus: maintainability: standards & comment-hygiene & sk-code:opencode alignment (no ephemeral ids in comments, durable WHY, idiom)

title: Review iteration template omits a field the validator requires
detail: The validator expands review iteration requirements to REVIEW_ITERATION_FIELDS when filesReviewed/findingsSummary are required; that list includes findingDetails and the validator rejects rows where findingDetails is not an array. The coupled leaf prompt canonical record and delta example omit findingDetails, so a normal agent following the template can emit a state-log row that fails post-dispatch validation.
claim: The deep-review leaf iteration output contract and post-dispatch validator disagree on whether findingDetails is required.
counterevidenceSought: Checked the command YAML and state JSONL reference; both require findingDetails, so the counterevidence supports validator-side intent but confirms prompt-pack drift.
alternativeExplanation: A runtime prompt assembly layer may add findingDetails outside the inspected template; if so, the template examples are still stale but the normal dispatch path may avoid the failure.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the dispatcher is shown to always inject findingDetails before validation, or if the prompt pack is no longer used by review leaf agents.
suggestedFix: Update the prompt pack canonical JSON and delta examples to include findingDetails: [] for no-finding iterations, or relax validator/state_jsonl/prompt-pack together if findingDetails is intentionally optional.

title: appendJsonlIfChangedAtomic is single-writer despite append-shaped API
detail: The exported helper is documented as appending a JSONL row, but it reads the whole file and atomically replaces it with old content plus the new row. Without a writer lock or O_APPEND, concurrent callers can lose one append. Current inspected call sites are single-loop telemetry paths, so this is a robustness/test-gap issue rather than an observed normal-use data-loss defect in this slice.
claim: The helper's API/documentation overstates append safety and does not encode its single-writer precondition.
counterevidenceSought: Current rg call sites are single-loop telemetry writes guarded away from fanout, so I did not find a current multi-process caller in this slice.
alternativeExplanation: The helper may be intentionally scoped to single-writer telemetry and named for atomic replacement rather than multi-writer append semantics.
downgradeTrigger: Drop if maintainers mark the helper private/single-writer-only and tests assert that contract, or if a lock/append-only implementation is added before any multi-process reuse.
suggestedFix: Rename/document it as single-writer replace-append, or introduce a lock-held/O_APPEND path before reusing it for multi-process ledgers.
